titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Leon Katz Papers
titlestmt.author: by Emily Komornik

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 66 published digital objects.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Describing Archives: A Content Standard

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday April 9, 2026 at 00:40

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Leon Katz Papers contains research files, writings, personal papers, professional papers, correspondence, subject files, photographs, printed materials, computer media, and audiovisual materials by or relating to Leon Katz.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 56-57 (student works): Restricted until 2087. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Box 84 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 85-89 (audiovisual media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Glenn Horowitz Bookseller, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight series: I. Research and Writings on Gertrude Stein, 1914-circa 2015 and undated. II. Professional and Personal Papers, 1950-2016 and undated. III. Correspondence and Subject Files, 1943-2016 and undated. IV. Other Papers, 1975-2016 and undated. V. Writings of Others, circa 1947-2009 and undated. VI. Photographs, circa 1950-2016 and undated. VII. Printed Materials, 1939-2010 and undated. VIII. Computer and Audiovisual Media, 1947-1998 and undated.

bioghist.head: Leon Katz (1919-2017)
bioghist.p: Leon Katz was an American playwright, dramaturg, and Professor Emeritus of Drama at Yale University, where he was co-chair of the Dramaturgy and Dramatic Criticism department from 1981 to 1989. He was a prominent scholar of Gertrude Stein, and as a doctoral candidate in 1948, Katz uncovered a previously obscure collection of Stein's notebooks in her archives at Yale, kept while she was writing The Making of Americans (1925). His transcriptions of the notebooks, followed by extensive interviews with Stein's partner Alice B. Toklas from 1952 to 1953, were the foundation of his 1963 dissertation, "The First Making of The Making of Americans: A Study Based on Gertrude Stein's Notebooks and Early Versions of Her Novel (1902-1908)." Throughout his life, Katz continued to work on his book, The Notebooks of Gertrude Stein, which he completed days before his death in 2017.

Katz was born on July 10, 1919 in New York City to Bernard Katz and Rachel Koslow. He graduated from the City College of New York in 1940 and studied at Columbia University, where he earned an M.A. in 1946 and a Ph.D. in English and Comparative Literature in 1962.

Katz was a professor of drama for over 60 years; he taught at Carnegie Mellon University from 1968 to 1977 and the University of Pittsburgh from 1977 to 1981, where he was a leading figure in Pittsburgh's theater scene and active in local alternative theater companies such as Theater Express and the 99 Cent Floating Theatre. After retiring from Yale, he taught at the University of California Los Angeles for 12 years. In 2004, he was appointed David G. Grey Distinguished Professor of Dramaturg for PlayMakers Repertory Company at the University of North Carolina. He was recognized for his contributions to teaching with an Association for Theatre in Higher Education lifetime achievement award in 2004.

Katz wrote over 50 plays, among them original works such as The Three Cuckolds (1986), The Son of Arlecchino (1986), and Beds (2000), and adaptations from other authors and playwrights including Dracula: Sabbat, The Dybbuk, Pinocchio, and the Marquis de Sade's Justine. In 1973, he wrote an opera based on Stein's The Making of Americans with composer Al Carmines. Four of his plays were published in the anthology Midnight Plays (1992). In the field of dramaturgy, Katz was renowned for his 1984 essay "The Compleat Dramaturg." He also served as resident dramaturg at the Mark Taper Forum in Los Angeles, where he contributed work on Tony Kushner's play Angels in America (1992).
Katz was married to Sadell Kasmere, an elementary school classmate, with whom he had two sons, Elia and Frederick. He died on January 23, 2017 in Encino, California.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Leon Katz Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Leon Katz originally organized his collection into four large groupings: Leon Katz Papers, Leon Katz-Gertrude Stein Archive, Katz's Files, and Other Materials. After receipt, library staff reviewed the housing and arrangement of the collection and made adjustments as necessary to meet collection management requirements. An archivist sorted the boxes into broader series, but made few changes to the folder-level arrangement of the archive, except where necessary to meet the library's best practices for processing manuscript collections. Library staff wrote the collection- and series-level description. Almost all description at the file level was transcribed from folder titles supplied by Katz, with minimal edits made by library staff. Titles in brackets were supplied by the archivist.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Leon Katz Papers consists of research files, writings, personal and professional papers, correspondence, subject files, photographs, printed materials, computer media, and audiovisual materials by or relating to Leon Katz. The collection documents Katz's lifelong study of Gertrude Stein; included are extensive research files, notes, drafts, and Stein's correspondence with notable associates, such as Mabel Dodge Luhan, Ernest Hemingway, Henri Matisse, Pablo Picasso, Leo Stein, Mabel Weeks, and Carl Van Vechten. It also includes a card index and transcripts of Katz's interviews with Stein's partner, Alice B. Toklas, as well as Katz's doctoral thesis about the earliest drafts of Stein's novel The Making of Americans, and drafts of his book The Notebooks of Gertrude Stein. Personal and professional papers contain Katz's other books, anthologies, plays, scripts, articles, and reviews by Katz, in addition to teaching files, artwork, and personal financial records. Correspondence and subject files include letters between Katz and notable individuals such as Meiling Cheng, Ulla Dydo, Donald Gallup, Peter Golub, Virgil Thomson, Alice Toklas, and his son, writer Elia Katz. Also present within the collection are translations, plays, books, and articles by other writers, including Conrad and Linda Bishop, Donald Freed, Georgi Iliev, Elia and Fred Katz, Rose Martula, and Orson Welles. Photographs contain images of Katz and his family, as well as images from theater productions. Printed materials contain books, serials, and broadsides. Computer and audiovisual media consist of compact discs, DVDs, floppy disks, videocassettes, audiocassettes, and audio discs.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Fifty books and four serial publications were removed for separate cataloging and can be accessed by searching the library's online catalog. A bibliography of these materials can be found in Appendix A.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Leon Katz Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

list.head: Books:
list.item: Aeschylus. The Eumenides. Translated by Hugh Lloyd-Jones. Englewood Cliffs, NJ: Prentice-Hall, Inc., 1970.
Aeschylus. The Oresteia Trilogy and Prometheus Bound. Translated by Michael Townsend. San Francisco: Chandler Publishing Company, 1966.
Agnon, S.Y. A Simple Story. Translated by Hillel Halkin. New York: Schocken Books, 1985.
Berlin, Isaiah. Against the Current: Essays in the History of Ideas. Edited by Henry Hardy. New York: Penguin Books, 1982.
Bradbury, Malcolm and James McFarlane, eds. Modernism: A Guide to European Literature, 1890-1930. New York: Penguin Books, 1976.
Burns, Edward, ed. Staying on Alone: Letters of Alice B. Toklas. New York: Liveright, 1973. (2 copies)
Chamberlin, Roy B. and Herman Feldman , eds . The Dartmouth Bible. Boston: Houghton Mifflin Company, 1950.

Modernism, 1885
-1917. New York: Oxford University Press, 1993.

Dydo, Ulla E., ed. A Stein Reader. Evanston, IL: Northwestern University Press, 1993.

Four Americans in Paris: The Collection of Gertrude Stein and Her Family. New York: The Museum of Modern Art, 1970.
Gide, André and Jean-Louis Barrault. The Trial: A Dramatization Based on Franz Kafka's Novel. Translated by Leon Katz. New York: Schocken Books, 1963
Grahn, Judy. Really Reading Gertrude Stein. Freedom, CA: The Crossing Press, 1989.
Gygax, Franziska. Gender and Genre in Gertrude Stein. Westport, CT: Greenwood Press, 1998.
Hobsbawm, E.J. The Age of Empire: 1875-1914. New York: Vintage Books, 1989.
The Holy Bible: Containing the Old and New Testaments, A Reference Edition. New York: American Bible Society , n.d.
Hubbell, George Shelton. Writing Documented Papers. New York: De Pamphilis Press, Inc., 1941.
Katz, Leon and Georgi Iliev, eds. Classical Monologues, From Aeschylus to Bernard Shaw, Volume 1: Younger Men's Roles. New York: Applause Theatre & Cinema Books, 2002.

Volume 4: From the Restoration to Bernard Shaw. New York: Applause Theatre & Cinema Books, 2004.
Katz, Leon. Cleaning the Augean Stables: Examining Drama's Strategies. Encino, CA: Leon Katz, 2012.

Katz, Leon. Midnight Plays
. Princeton: Princeton University Press, 1992.

Kostelanetz, Richard, ed. The Yale Gertrude Stein. New Haven and London: Yale University Press, 1980.
Meyer, Steven. Irresistible Dictation: Gertrude Stein and the Correlations of Writing and Science. Stanford: Stanford University Press, 2001.
Miles, Jack. God: A Biography. New York: Vintage Books, 1996.
Musil, Robert. Five Women. Translated by Eithne Wilkins and Ernst Kaiser. Boston: Verba Mundi, 1999.
Pivano, Fernanda. Gertrude Stein: Pioniera Di Un Secolo. n.p., n.d.

Reid, B.L. Art
By
Subtraction: A Dissenting Opinion of Gertrude Stein. Norman: University of Oklahoma Press, 1958.

Rubin, William, ed. Pablo Picasso: A Retrospective. New York: The Museum of Modern Art, 1980.
Shattuck, Roger. The Banquet Years: The Origins of the Avant Garde in France, 1885 to World War I. New York: Vintage Books, 196 8.
Souham, Diana. Gertrude and Alice. San Francisco: HarperCollins, 1992.
Stein, Gertrude. The Autobiography of Alice B. Toklas. New York: The Literary Guild, 1933.

Watson, Steven. Strange Bedfellows: The First American Avant-Garde. New York: Abbeville Press Publishers, 1991.
Wineapple, Brenda. Sister Brother: Gertrude & Leo Stein. Baltimore: The Johns Hopkins University Press, 1996.

The Yale Review 100, no. 3 (July 2012).

did.unittitle: Research and Writings on Gertrude Stein

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains research files, writings, and a card index relating to Leon Katz's lifelong study of Gertrude Stein. Research files include correspondence, subject files, notes, notebooks, drafts, articles, and materials gathered from the Gertrude Stein and Alice B. Toklas papers and Gertrude Stein and Alice B. Toklas collection at the Beinecke Library. The card index contains notes organized chronologically, topically, and by individual, as well as notes from Katz's interviews with Toklas from 1952 to 1953. Notable friends and associates of Stein represented in the materials include Mary Berenson, Etta Cone, Kenyon Cox, Mabel Dodge Luhan, Ulla Dydo, Donald Gallup, Ernest Hemingway, Henri Matisse, Pablo Picasso, Alfred Stieglitz, Alice Toklas, Carl Van Vechten, Mabel Weeks, and her brother, art critic Leo Stein. Writings on Stein contain Katz's 1963 dissertation "The Making of The Making of Americans: A Study Based on Gertrude Stein's Notebooks and Early Versions of Her Novel (1902-1908)," his book The Notebooks of Gertrude Stein , and a printout of A Long Gay Book , annotated by Katz.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Research Files, Card Index, and Writings on Gertrude Stein. Materials maintain the order found at time of acquisition.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Materials related to Gertrude Stein can also be found in Series IV. Other Papers and Series VII. Printed Materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains writings, publishing correspondence, teaching files, personal financial records, and artwork by or relating to Leon Katz. Included are drafts, notes, proofs, revisions, and excisions of Katz's plays, books, articles, and reviews. Among the works featured are the plays A Death in Astapovo, Beds, Dracula: Sabbat , an adaptation of Finnegan's Wake, The Son of Arlecchino , and Swellfoot's Tears ; and the anthologies Classical Monologues and Cleaning the Augean Stables: Dramaturgical Notes for Playwrights . Teaching files contain coursework, syllabi, outlines, lecture slides, class notes, student works, and reading lists. Personal financial records include bank statements, checkbooks, and tax returns. Artwork contains drawings, portraits, and paintings by unknown artists. Also present are three pairs of Katz's eyeglasses.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five subseries: Writings, Teaching Files, Personal Financial, Artwork, and Eyeglasses.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Correspondence can also be found in Series III. Correspondence and Subject Files. Writings can also be found in Series IV. Other Papers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four groupings: Plays, Books, Film and Television Scripts, and Reviews. Materials within each grouping are arranged alphabetically by title.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 56-57 (student works): Restricted until 2087. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Correspondence and Subject Files. Arranged alphabetically by correspondent or folder title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence between Katz, friends, family, and professional contacts. Notable correspondents include Betty Ann Besch, Meiling Cheng, Donald Gallup, Peter Golub, Elia Katz, Tony Kushner, Virgil Thomson, and Alice Toklas. Also present are subject files for Applause Book Publishers, the Authors Guild, Brown University, Emory University, The Independent Eye, the Living Theatre, and the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Correspondence can also be found in Series II. Professional and Personal Papers.

did.unittitle: American Theatre Collection, Inc., business plan and pro forma, financial statements

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Not eligible for digitization. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Talks on Western Drama, complimentary copies
Veterans' aid and attendance research for Leon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains drafts, notes, proofs, and excisions from articles, scripts, musical scores, and books by Leon Katz and others.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Writings by Katz can also be found in Series I. Research and Writings on Gertrude Stein and Series II. Professional and Personal Papers. Works by other writers can also be found in Series V. Writings of Others.

did.unittitle: 27: An Opera in Five Acts , music by Ricky Ian Gordon, libretto by Royce Vavrek
"An Analysis of Julius Caesar" by Casey LeSawyer
Everybody who was Anybody: A Biography of Gertrude Stein by Janet Hobhouse
Gertrude Stein's notes on Doctor Faustus Lights the Lights [Katz's notebook]
The Notebooks of Michael Mabius , a novel by Elia Katz
[Notes for "The Decline of Certainties, Medieval to Jacobean"]
[Page proof of index from an unidentified book]
[Printed page excerpts, class notes and journal entries]
Thank you note from The Independent Eye
Viking Economics: How the Scandinavians Got it Right, and How We Can, Too by George Lakey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains translations, articles, books, reviews, scripts, and proposals of other writers, sent to Katz for his feedback, as well as related correspondence. Authors include Conrad and Linda Bishop, Donald Freed, Georgi Iliev, Elia and Fred Katz, Rose Martula, Brendan Ward, and Orson Welles.

did.unittitle: Alexander, Tania and Suter, Tim, translation of "Vassa Zheleznova"
Anderson, Nina Shannon, "Chavez: Going Towards the Light"
Berman, Paul and Graybill, Chris, "Joe's Odyssey to the Catbird Seat"
"Goners: A Horror Show in Two Acts," first draft
"Last Days of the Lost and Found"
"Marie Antoinette: A Chronicle in Two Acts"
"Mine Alone: A Play in Two Acts"
"Smitty's News: A Play in Two Acts"
"Pied A Terre: An Original Television Series" with Michelle Watkins
DeVoti, Emily, "The Turn of the Screw: An Adaptation"
Fava, Giuseppe, "Violence: Drama in Three Acts" translated into English
"The Einstein Plan" rehearsal draft, not for distribution
"Intimations of Immortality: From the Letters of Sabina Spielrein, Carl G. Jung and Sigmund Freud"

title.part: The Life and Death of Mr. Iliev

did.unittitle: Correspondence and articles from Gregory Armstrong, Bantam Books
"Everything You Need to Know About Making…an $8,000 Movie" annotated
"Introduction to the Method of Paul Vallery"
The Johns Hopkins University Conferring of Degrees

title.part: The Last Days of Michael Mabius

did.unittitle: A Structuralist Anthology, [draft annotated by Leon Katz]
"When My Uncle Was on His Death Bed"
"Giant Brains: The Product of the Second Industrial Revolution"
"Is Necessity the Mother of Intention?" with Jerrold Katz
M., Rina, "Mythos Act I: the Revenge (After the Death of Agamemnon)"
Rose's play, first and second drafts
Miller, David, "Dr. Faustus Lights the Lights" music for Gertrude Stein's Libretto
Moad, Rosalind, "1914-1916: Years of Innovation in Gertrude Stein's Writing"
Olmos, Matthew Paul, "I Put the Fear of Mexico in 'Em"
Peters, Robert, "Mengele's Uterus, The Sex Life of J. Edgar Hoover, A Fairy's Tale"
Saunders, Nicholas and Dwyer, Frank, translation of "The Sea Gull (Chaikra): A Comedy in Four Acts"
"Another Sideshow Attraction" draft, with suggested scenes by Katz
"Short Stories About the End of the World" parts 1 and 2 of 6
"Youth-In-Asia: A Short Play" and "The Scream: Theatre in One Act"
"Dumbo Arts International Proposal, Center for International Arts in the Community of Dumbo, Brooklyn" with Daniel Kadin
Welles, Orson, The Dreamers: A Film

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains photographs of Leon Katz, his friends, and family, as well as production stills from Chavez: Going Towards the Light , Paradise Now , and other plays staged at the Living Theatre. Also included is a photograph of Gertrude Stein taken by Carl Van Vechten, given to Leon Katz by Edward Burns in 1972.

did.unittitle: Gertrude Stein, photographed by Carl Van Vechten, 1934, to Katz from Edward Burns
The Play's the Thing and Chavez: Going Towards the Light

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains books, serial publications, and broadsides pertaining to or featuring Katz's work, especially his writings on Gertrude Stein.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries by format: Books, Broadsides, and Serials. Arranged alphabetically by title.

did.unittitle: Agamemnon, Aeschylus , edited and translated by Peter D. Arnott
AHTOH ПABЛOBИЧ ЧEXOB B TEATPE [Russian book of photographs, various performances]
Draw Your Will Yourself…Now! , David Lehman
"Favored Strangers:" Gertrude Stein and Her Family , Linda Wagner-Martin
Gertrude Stein in Pieces , Richard Bridgman
Gertrude Stein: Modernism and the Problem of Genius , Barbara Will
Gertrude Stein: The Language That Rises , Ulla E. Dydo and William Rice
Henri-Pierre Roche: An Introduction , Carlon Lake and Linda Ashton
Lectures in America: The Great Writer Discusses the Concepts of Art , Gertrude Stein
The Letters of Gertrude Stein and Thornton Wilder , edited by Edward Burns and Ulla Dydo, with William Rice
The Making of a Modernist: Gertrude Stein from "Three Lives" to "Tender Buttons," Jayne L. Walker
The Making of Americans , Gertrude Stein, with foreword by William H. Gass
The Making of Americans: An Opera and a Play , Leon Katz
Matisse, Picasso, and Gertrude Stein: With Two Shorter Stories , Gertrude Stein
A Primer for the Understanding of Gertrude Stein , edited by Robert Bartlett Haas
Reading Gertrude Stein: Body, Text, Gnosis , Lisa Ruddick
Selected Letters of Virgil Thomson , edited by Tim Page and Vanessa Weeks Page
Selected Writings of Gertrude Stein , edited by Carl Van Vechten
A Stein Reader , edited by Ulla Dydo
Stein, Writings 1932-1946 , edited by Catharine R. Stimpson and Harriet Chessman
The Trial: A Dramatization Based on Franz Kafka's Novel , Andre Gide and Jean-Louis Barrault, translated by Leon Katz
Anonymous photo of Gertrude Stein, 1918, from the collection of Edward Burns
Art and Technology: Los Angeles County Museum of Art
Barnes & Noble Welcomes Leon Katz, Author of Midnight Plays
"Death of Queen Elizabeth" copyrighted 1887 by Gebbie & Company
A Death in Astapovo: The Last Days of Tolstoy , at the University of Pittsburgh Theatre
Classics in Contest Festival: Comedy and Tragedy in the Italian Tradition
Hamlet Act I Scene IV, "The Platform by the Sea at Elsinore," copyrighted 1887 by Gebbie & Company
Maxim Gorsky's The Lower Depths , Directed by Leon Katz
[Photographic print of two women and a letter in an unidentified hand]
Pittsburgh Laboratory Theatre Presents Swellfoot's Tears, by Leon Katz
The West Coast Premiere of the Marquis de Sade's Justine , Adapted by Leon Katz

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains computer and audiovisual media including compact discs, DVDs, floppy disks, videocassettes, audiocassettes, and audio discs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized in two subseries: Computer Media, and Audiovisual Media.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 84 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 85-89 (audiovisual media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

Vol. IV 9-21, La Poetica [labeled two disks, only contains one]
Vol. IV 343-353, Gololoni and Gobri [labeled two disks, only contains one]
Vol. V 9-15, The CDA in Paris [labeled two disks, only contains one]
Paul Robeson, Ballad for Americans, for Leon
